tasks.title: Persistent RunRepository adapter shipped and verified
tasks.description: Completed Stage 1 / Epic 1 run persistence by replacing bootstrap's default `InMemoryRunRepository` path with a SQLAlchemy-backed durable adapter, persisting full `RunRecord` payloads to `.local/state/runs.sqlite3` by default, exposing `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL`, and adding restart-persistence coverage in the API and application tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the durable run-storage implementation, verified it with `pytest` (`13 passed`), and committed the change as `d6e3041`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets research-foundation success criteria for restart-safe runs and reproducible experiment records.

tasks.title: RunRepository deployment database wiring shipped
tasks.description: Completed the deployed-environment RunRepository follow-up by adding the Postgres driver dependency, enabling SQLAlchemy `pool_pre_ping`, pointing Compose `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL` at the `postgres` service with health-gated startup, adding bootstrap coverage for a configured non-SQLite URL, and documenting the Compose/Postgres behavior.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the Postgres deployment wiring, verified API tests (`15 passed`), and committed the change as `aa2b6f4`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closes the deployment-database extension of the research-foundation persistence work without reopening the completed adapter implementation.

tasks.title: Smoke test Compose startup against Postgres-backed run storage
tasks.description: Run an end-to-end Docker Compose startup on a machine with Docker installed to confirm the API waits for Postgres health, boots successfully with `AKRA_TRADER_RUNS_DATABASE_URL` pointed at the `postgres` service, and persists run storage without SQLite fallback.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply called this the only unresolved follow-up from the deployment-database work, but execution still needs a Docker-capable environment outside the current workspace.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Provides the remaining deployment validation for research-foundation persistence without adding new product scope.

tasks.title: Binance-backed candle ingestion and sync status shipped
tasks.description: Completed the Track A / Epic 2 market-data foundation by replacing the default seeded path with a SQL-backed Binance ingestion adapter, persisting OHLCV coverage with freshness reuse, surfacing `sync_status` / lag / gap signals through the API and web UI, and keeping `seeded` only as an offline test fallback.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the Binance-backed ingestion implementation, verified API and web coverage, and committed the change as `74c3a39`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly targets the research-foundation success criterion that market data be Binance-backed with lag visibility and quality checks.

tasks.title: Binance market-data sync now runs as a scheduled background job
tasks.description: Completed the request-path sync removal by moving Binance refresh into a scheduled `MarketDataSyncJob`, wiring startup/shutdown through the FastAPI lifespan, adding sync interval/timeframe configuration, and covering both unsynced request behavior and job lifecycle with API tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the scheduled Binance sync migration, verified it with `pytest` (`27 passed`), and committed the change as `f722373`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the research-foundation market-data lane from request-time freshness checks to predictable ingestion operations suitable for real trading workflows.

tasks.title: Binance historical backfill beyond rolling sync windows shipped
tasks.description: Completed the historical coverage follow-up by teaching Binance background sync to keep the recent window current and then walk backward in batches until a configurable historical candle target is reached, wiring `AKRA_TRADER_MARKET_DATA_HISTORICAL_CANDLE_LIMIT` through bootstrap/config, and adding API/bootstrap coverage for expanding stored history beyond the rolling sync window.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the historical Binance backfill implementation, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`), and committed the change as `b64edf2`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the research-foundation ingestion lane from short-horizon freshness into reproducible historical coverage needed for credible research and replay.

tasks.title: Binance historical backfill target and completion now exposed in ops status
tasks.description: Completed the operator-facing backfill status follow-up by extending the API `InstrumentStatus` shape with historical target, completion ratio, and completion flag fields, populating them for Binance-backed symbols while keeping seeded payloads compatible, and surfacing both summary tiles and per-instrument progress bars in the control-room market-data table.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the backfill visibility work, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `69893ae`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room goal that operators can tell whether Binance data is only recently refreshed or fully backfilled to the configured historical target.

tasks.title: Binance ops now separates backfill count completion from contiguous coverage quality
tasks.description: Completed the ops quality follow-up by splitting Binance instrument status into distinct count-completion and contiguous-quality views, exposing missing-candle-aware quality fields through the API model, surfacing separate control-room summary tiles plus per-instrument Count and Quality columns, and locking in the 'count complete but gap exists' case with API and web verification.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the count-versus-quality split in ops status, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `4da9d13`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Pushes the control-room ops surface from simple backfill progress toward operator-trustworthy data quality signals about whether historical coverage is actually continuous enough to rely on.

tasks.title: Exact Binance gap windows are now shown with backfill quality status
tasks.description: Completed the gap-window diagnostics follow-up by deriving concrete missing candle ranges from Binance candle timestamps, exposing them as `backfill_gap_windows` in instrument status, and rendering precise `start -> end (missing_count)` ranges in the control-room UI with API and web coverage for gap-free and single-gap cases.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the exact gap-window surface, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`28 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `589bbdc`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room trust surface from coarse quality alarms toward operator-actionable diagnostics by showing where historical Binance coverage actually breaks.

tasks.title: Fragmented Binance gap windows now collapse into a readable ops summary
tasks.description: Completed the fragmented-gap follow-up by collapsing long Binance gap-window lists in the control-room quality cell, preserving a few recent exact windows while rolling older fragments into a compact summary line with range and missing-count context, plus matching UI treatment for the collapsed state.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the fragmented gap-window rollup, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `e03deb3`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the control-room gap diagnostics operator-usable as Binance history grows more fragmented, so the new quality surface stays readable instead of turning into raw noise.

tasks.title: On-demand expand/collapse control for rolled-up Binance gap windows shipped
tasks.description: Completed the rolled-gap follow-up by adding an inline `Show all N gaps` / collapse control to the control-room quality cell so rolled-up Binance gap windows stay collapsed by default but can be expanded on demand to inspect the full exact list, with matching inline control styling in the web UI.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the expand/collapse control, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `85ffbdb`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the rolled-up gap diagnostics so the control room stays readable by default while preserving access to exact Binance missing-window detail when operators need to investigate.

tasks.title: Expanded Binance gap-window rows now persist across refreshes
tasks.description: Completed the refresh-persistence follow-up by lifting rolled-up Binance gap-window expand/collapse state to app scope, keying it by instrument and timeframe, persisting it in `localStorage`, and verifying the web app with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` before committing `8e4e4ca`.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the refresh-safe expand/collapse persistence work, verified it in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `8e4e4ca` (`feat(ops): persist expanded gap rows`).
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the control-room gap diagnostics so operator investigation survives periodic UI refreshes instead of collapsing long Binance gap lists mid-review.

tasks.title: Stale persisted Binance gap-window expansion keys are pruned
tasks.description: Completed the stale-key cleanup follow-up by filtering persisted Binance gap-window expand/collapse state against the current ops payload so removed or no-longer-rolled-up rows are dropped from `localStorage` during market-data status refreshes instead of accumulating obsolete keys.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the stale-key pruning work in `apps/web`, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build`, and committed the change as `a53a1a9` (`feat(ops): prune stale gap row state`).
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the control-room gap-window persistence trustworthy over time by ensuring operator-facing refresh state stays aligned with the currently tracked Binance rows.

tasks.title: Persisted control-room UI state is now versioned before storage scope grows
tasks.description: Completed the control-room persistence follow-up by wrapping stored UI state in a versioned `control-room-ui-state` envelope, preserving fallback reads from the legacy `akra-trader-gap-window-expansion` key, and writing only the versioned payload while removing the old key.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the control-room UI state versioning work, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `b6fa551` (`feat(ops): version control room ui state`).
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps control-room persistence maintainable as more operator-facing UI preferences become durable, so future state additions do not strand incompatible browser payloads.

tasks.title: Add explicit migration handling when a second persisted control-room preference lands
tasks.description: Once another control-room UI preference is introduced into the shared storage envelope, add an explicit version-to-version migration path and coverage so stored operator preferences upgrade intentionally instead of relying only on the current legacy fallback from the pre-envelope gap-row map.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply said the remaining follow-up is explicit migration handling for a future second UI preference, but that work should stay a proposal until the broader persisted preference scope actually exists.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps the control-room persistence lane forward-compatible by reserving real migration work for the point where multiple durable operator preferences share one versioned browser payload.

tasks.title: Broaden Binance symbol and timeframe prewarm configuration
tasks.description: Expand configuration beyond the default tracked set so operators can declare additional Binance symbols, timeframes, and startup prewarm coverage targets, with validation and tests that keep ingestion scope explicit.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply named broader symbol/timeframe configuration as a secondary follow-up, but scheduled syncing is the more immediate queue-head task.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the same research-foundation ingestion lane while keeping configurable market coverage separate from the scheduling implementation.

tasks.title: Control-room run lineage panel shipped
tasks.description: Completed the control-room lineage follow-up by surfacing `run.provenance.market_data` inside each run card, including provider, sync status, candle count, timeframe, venue and symbols, requested vs effective windows, last sync time, and lineage issues, with dedicated UI styling for provenance detail.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the control-room lineage UI, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `73edd55`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the control-room goal that operator surfaces explain what data a run used, how complete it was, and whether it is safe to trust.

tasks.title: Per-symbol market-data lineage capture shipped for multi-symbol runs
tasks.description: Completed the provenance follow-up by extending run lineage beyond one aggregate `market_data` record so native and delegated multi-symbol runs now persist symbol-scoped lineage in `market_data_by_symbol` while keeping the aggregate field for compatibility, with API/application/freqtrade coverage and serialization tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-symbol lineage implementation, verified `apps/api` with `pytest` (`24 passed`), and committed the change as `0d8d44b`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the research-foundation reproducibility work by making dataset provenance precise enough for multi-symbol comparisons and audits.

tasks.title: Per-symbol lineage now surfaced in control-room run details
tasks.description: Completed the control-room follow-up by rendering `provenance.market_data_by_symbol` in run details so multi-symbol runs now expose symbol-scoped sync status, candle counts, effective windows, last sync time, and lineage issues alongside the aggregate lineage block, while older runs gracefully fall back to aggregate-only display.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-symbol lineage control-room surface, verified it with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and committed the change as `a71d3a9`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the control-room goal that operators can inspect and trust multi-symbol run provenance from one surface, with backward-compatible fallback for older runs that only retain aggregate lineage.

tasks.title: Strategy lifecycle metadata and version filters shipped across catalog and runs
tasks.description: Completed the Epic 3 metadata follow-up by extending `StrategyMetadata` with lifecycle and version-lineage fields, carrying lineage into stored strategy snapshots, exposing lane/lifecycle/version filtering through the strategies API, adding run-history filtering by `strategy_id` and `strategy_version`, and surfacing lifecycle, version lineage, and parameter snapshots in the control-room strategy and run views.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the shared strategy metadata and run-filtering work and verified it with `pytest` (`32 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Covers the strategy-platform success criteria around explicit runtime lane metadata and comparable version lifecycle.

tasks.title: Run comparison workflow for native and reference benchmarks shipped
tasks.description: Completed the Stage 2 compare-runs workflow by adding ordered multi-run lookup plus `GET /api/runs/compare`, returning side-by-side comparison payloads for total return, max drawdown, win rate, trade count, run notes, lane/status metadata, and strategy/version context, then wiring the control room to select up to four runs, auto-pick a native versus NFI baseline pair, and render baseline deltas with lane visibility.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the compare-runs workflow end to end, verified it with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`34 passed`), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run build`, and pushed commit `d36c99b` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Directly advances the research-foundation success criteria around reproducible experiments and straightforward comparison across strategy versions and benchmark lanes.

tasks.title: Persist reference-run provenance and artifact links for benchmark comparisons
tasks.description: Completed the benchmark-comparison provenance follow-up by persisting reference catalog snapshots into reference-lane run provenance, preserving benchmark-specific provenance and deterministic artifact paths in comparison payloads, teaching the Freqtrade adapter to retain expected result/log locations even when `freqtrade` is unavailable, and exposing the new provenance across control-room run and comparison views.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the reference-run provenance and artifact-link follow-up, verified it with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`34 passed`), `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run build`, and pushed commit `fe96a26` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Supports the reference-lanes success criteria that reference runs stay explicit and traceable rather than becoming hidden native equivalents.

tasks.title: Typed benchmark artifacts now back reference-lane provenance
tasks.description: Completed the typed benchmark artifact follow-up by adding a `BenchmarkArtifact` model to reference-run provenance and comparison payloads, teaching the Freqtrade adapter to classify deterministic artifact paths into typed benchmark artifacts while preserving backward-compatible flat `artifact_paths`, surfacing the richer artifact metadata in normal and comparison payloads plus the control room, and adding backend assertions for typed artifact kinds.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the typed benchmark artifact implementation, verified `apps/api` with `.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`34 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `f2a9354` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference-lanes benchmark surface from generic artifact links toward operator-meaningful benchmark evidence without collapsing external-runtime runs into native semantics.

tasks.title: Typed benchmark artifacts now ingest Freqtrade manifests and summaries
tasks.description: Completed the manifest-enrichment follow-up by extending `BenchmarkArtifact` with structured summary metadata and `summary_source_path`, teaching the Freqtrade reference adapter to enrich typed artifacts from `.meta.json` manifests, result `.json` summaries, sibling manifests for `.zip` snapshots, and backtest result roots, then surfacing the normalized summary fields through stored provenance, comparison payloads, and control-room artifact rows with synthetic parsing coverage.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the manifest and summary enrichment work, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`36 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `ecc1a78` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Keeps reference-lane benchmark evidence explicit and operator-meaningful by grounding typed artifacts in real Freqtrade outputs instead of convention-only path heuristics.

tasks.title: Richer nested Freqtrade benchmark sections are now parsed into artifact summaries
tasks.description: Completed the nested-summary follow-up by reworking the Freqtrade artifact parser around the actual export layout (`metadata[strategy_name]`, `strategy[strategy_name]`, `strategy_comparison`, and nested strategy sections), adding structured `sections` to `BenchmarkArtifact`, improving ratio normalization for UI-ready percentages, surfacing nested benchmark sections in the control room, and replacing synthetic tests with fixtures mirroring real nested Freqtrade JSON.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the nested Freqtrade summary parsing work, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`36 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `9b4e75f` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from normalized artifact summaries toward benchmark-native semantics by capturing the nested Freqtrade structures that operators compare directly.

tasks.title: Inspect Freqtrade zip artifacts directly for benchmark provenance
tasks.description: Completed the direct zip-inspection follow-up by teaching the Freqtrade reference adapter to open benchmark `.zip` artifacts directly, extract provenance from embedded result/config JSON plus strategy bundles and source files, record export presence for wallet/market-change/signals/rejected/exited artifacts, surface the new `zip_contents`, `zip_config`, and `zip_strategy_bundle` sections through benchmark artifact payloads and control-room labels, and cover both direct zip inspection and root-level provenance fallback with adapter tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the direct Freqtrade zip-artifact inspection work, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `9347fbf` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from parsed result summaries toward fuller benchmark provenance by reading the packaged Freqtrade evidence operators actually export and inspect.

tasks.title: Embedded Freqtrade feather and pickle exports now contribute structural provenance
tasks.description: Completed the binary-export inspection follow-up by teaching the Freqtrade reference adapter to read embedded `*_market_change.feather`, `*_wallet.feather`, `*_signals.pkl`, `*_rejected.pkl`, and `*_exited.pkl` members inside benchmark zip snapshots, extract structural provenance such as row/frame counts, column lists, time ranges, strategy/pair counts, wallet currency summaries, and preview entries, keep parsing defensive when binary members are unreadable, add the required `joblib` and `pyarrow` runtime dependencies, and surface the new binary-export sections through control-room labels.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the embedded Freqtrade binary-export inspection, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `0322740` to `main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference benchmark lane from zip-level inventory into deeper benchmark-native provenance by reading the packaged binary exports that still remain opaque to operators and comparison views.

tasks.title: Decode richer semantics from embedded Freqtrade binary exports
tasks.description: Completed the semantic-decoding follow-up by extending the Freqtrade zip inspector to read richer meaning from embedded binary exports, including market-change best/worst pairs and positive/negative counts, wallet portfolio start/end/high/low quote values plus per-currency latest quote previews, and signal/rejected/exited previews with `enter_tag`, reject-reason, and exit-reason breakdown counts, while keeping unreadable members non-fatal and covering the new behavior with real in-memory feather/joblib payload tests plus control-room labels.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the richer Freqtrade binary-export semantic decoding, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `117bc55` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from structural binary-export provenance toward richer benchmark-native semantics that operators can compare directly without opening raw Freqtrade artifacts.

tasks.title: Higher-level benchmark narratives from decoded Freqtrade exports shipped
tasks.description: Completed the benchmark narrative follow-up by synthesizing decoded Freqtrade zip exports into artifact/root `benchmark_story` sections that summarize run headline, market breadth, wallet trajectory, signal bias, rejection friction, exit posture, and pair concentration, then surfacing those readable labels in the web artifact cards and locking both snapshot/root narrative outputs into API tests.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the higher-level Freqtrade benchmark narrative synthesis, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`37 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `07b6caf` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Pushes the reference benchmark lane past raw decoded sections into operator-usable benchmark narratives that improve quick comparison without collapsing external-runtime evidence into native analytics.

tasks.title: Explain native-vs-reference benchmark divergence with comparison-level narratives
tasks.description: Completed the comparison-narrative follow-up by extending native-vs-reference run comparison payloads with structured comparison-level `narratives`, teaching the application layer to classify native/reference pairings and synthesize divergence headlines, summaries, and supporting bullets with fallback to reference benchmark artifact summaries, then surfacing the resulting narrative cards above the metric table in the control room with API and application regression coverage.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Advances the reference benchmark lane from isolated benchmark storytelling into direct native-versus-reference interpretation so operators can understand divergence without manually reconciling separate summaries.

tasks.title: Rank multi-run divergence narratives so comparisons surface the top insight first
tasks.description: Completed the multi-run ranking follow-up by scoring comparison-level divergence narratives with `insight_score`, annotating each narrative with `rank` and `is_primary`, ordering multi-run insights by return, drawdown, win-rate, trade-flow, and native-vs-reference context, and surfacing the top-ranked narrative first in the control room with regression coverage for both application ordering and API payload fields.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the multi-run divergence narrative ranking, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`39 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `6ea5716` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference benchmark comparison surface from per-run divergence explanations into operator-prioritized storytelling for multi-run benchmark sessions where the most important divergence should appear first.

tasks.title: Make divergence narrative ranking regime-aware by comparison intent
tasks.description: Completed the comparison-intent follow-up by adding explicit comparison `intent` support across the compare API and control-room toolbar, teaching the application layer to rank divergence narratives differently for `benchmark_validation`, `execution_regression`, and `strategy_tuning`, and locking in regime-specific ranking flips with application and API regression coverage.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the regime-aware ranking implementation, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`39 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `55e0735` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference benchmark comparison surface from one-size-fits-all insight ordering into intent-aware prioritization so operators see the most relevant divergence first for the kind of comparison they are running.

tasks.title: Make comparison narrative text itself intent-aware
tasks.description: Completed the intent-aware narrative-text follow-up by changing comparison-level titles, summaries, and supporting bullets to speak differently for `benchmark_validation`, `execution_regression`, and `strategy_tuning`, so validation emphasizes benchmark evidence, regression emphasizes execution drift, and tuning emphasizes optimization tradeoffs, with regression coverage for intent-specific wording and multi-run intent flips.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the intent-aware narrative-text implementation in `application.py`, verified `apps/api` with `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest` (`39 passed`) plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `4144177` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on intent-aware ranking by making the benchmark comparison narrative itself speak to the operator's comparison goal, so reference-lane divergence interpretation changes meaningfully with the selected regime instead of only changing order.

tasks.title: Make comparison metric annotations intent-aware too
tasks.description: Completed the metric-annotation follow-up by extending comparison metric rows with row-level `annotation` plus per-run `delta_annotations`, teaching the application layer to emit intent-specific metric copy for `benchmark_validation`, `execution_regression`, and `strategy_tuning`, updating the comparison table to render row annotations and backend-provided delta text, and locking in the new payload and wording with API/application regression coverage.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the intent-aware benchmark comparison surface from narrative copy into the metric table itself, so operators get consistent comparison framing in both prose and quantitative annotations.

tasks.title: Make comparison visual emphasis intent-aware

tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the intent-aware comparison copy by making the benchmark metric surface visually encode the comparison goal too, so operators do not have to rely on text alone to interpret drift versus optimization signals.

tasks.title: Add intent-aware iconography and compact legend cues to comparison mode
tasks.description: Completed the intent-aware comparison cue follow-up by adding intent-specific icons to the comparison header and mode chip, introducing a compact legend for current intent, baseline run, best metric, and top insight cues, reusing the same legend treatment in the featured insight area, and styling all three comparison intents with consistent swatches and iconography in the web UI.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the intent-aware comparison visual system by adding faster-recognition cues so benchmark-comparison mode stays legible even when operators scan the panel before reading detailed text.

tasks.title: Intent-aware hover states and tooltip copy shipped for comparison cues
tasks.description: Completed the intent-aware comparison cue explanation follow-up by adding comparison-mode tooltip copy for intent, legend, featured insight, baseline card, and emphasized metric cues, wiring those explanations into the comparison UI, and extending the same cue system with stronger intent-aware hover emphasis and tooltip styling in the web app.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the intent-aware hover and tooltip implementation in `App.tsx` and `styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `2b78321` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed comparison legend cues by making the benchmark-comparison emphasis self-explaining on hover, so operators can interpret why the UI is calling attention to a result without inferring the meaning from styling alone.

tasks.title: Keyboard-focus tooltip and focus-ring treatment shipped for comparison cues
tasks.description: Completed the keyboard accessibility follow-up by making comparison intent chips, legend items, featured insight cues, the baseline run card, and emphasized comparison cells focusable when they expose cue copy, extending the existing tooltip path so the same explanations appear on keyboard focus, and adding visible focus-ring treatment tuned separately for table-cell cues in the web app.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the keyboard-focus tooltip and focus-ring implementation in `App.tsx` and `styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `6084107` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed comparison cue hover system by making the same benchmark-comparison explanations and emphasis accessible from keyboard navigation, reducing reliance on mouse-only discovery for reference-lane comparisons.

tasks.title: Upgrade comparison cue tooltips to announced accessible tooltip semantics
tasks.description: Completed the accessible-tooltip follow-up by replacing CSS/native-only comparison cue hints with explicit tooltip DOM nodes, linking cue triggers to stable `aria-describedby` targets across intent chips, legend items, baseline cards, featured insight cues, narrative cards, and emphasized comparison cells, and adding panel-local hover/focus/Escape tooltip state so assistive technology and keyboard users receive the same announced explanations.

tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed keyboard-accessible comparison cue treatment by moving tooltip behavior from hover/focus styling alone to explicit assistive-technology-readable semantics, improving operator accessibility for reference-lane comparisons.

tasks.title: Make comparison tooltips place intelligently near viewport and scroller edges
tasks.description: Completed the tooltip-placement follow-up by measuring active comparison cue targets and tooltip bubbles at runtime, flipping placement above targets when needed, clamping horizontal placement against the viewport, constraining tooltip width to the visible `.comparison-table-wrap` scroller, recomputing on resize and scroll, and updating fixed-position tooltip/arrow styling so semantic comparison cues stay readable near viewport and table edges.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the edge-aware comparison tooltip placement in `App.tsx` and `styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `554c037` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed accessible tooltip semantics by making reference-lane comparison explanations stay visible and readable at narrow viewport and scroller boundaries instead of trading accessibility gains for clipped presentation.

tasks.title: Reduce comparison tooltip jitter across dense metric cells
tasks.description: Completed the dense-table tooltip stability follow-up by adding metric-cell-specific hover-intent timing, delaying mouse-triggered tooltip open slightly, delaying close briefly on leave, canceling pending open/close timers when focus or Escape takes over, and keeping non-table comparison cues plus keyboard-triggered tooltip behavior immediate.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the metric-cell hover-intent stability work in `App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `d32bd81` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed edge-aware tooltip placement by making dense reference-lane comparison tables feel stable during pointer movement instead of trading readable placement for hover jitter.

tasks.title: Suppress tooltip swaps during quick horizontal sweeps across comparison cells
tasks.description: Completed the directional pointer-intent follow-up by sampling recent metric-cell pointer movement, detecting quick horizontally dominant sweeps across adjacent comparison cells, and applying a longer hover-open delay only during those sweeps so the prior tooltip stays stable while preserving immediate keyboard focus and non-metric cue behavior.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the directional pointer-intent sweep suppression in `App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `e9d8fb9` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed hover-intent stabilization by making dense reference-lane comparison tables distinguish deliberate inspection from fast row-wise scanning, further reducing tooltip churn in the highest-noise interaction path.

tasks.title: Metric-row-aware comparison tooltip sweep suppression shipped
tasks.description: Completed the row-aware sweep suppression follow-up by tracking the active metric row alongside recent pointer samples, limiting horizontal sweep suppression persistence to the current metric row, clearing suppression when the pointer jumps into a different row, and preserving existing keyboard plus non-metric tooltip behavior in the comparison UI.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the metric-row-aware sweep suppression in `App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `02314cf` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed directional sweep suppression by aligning dense comparison-table tooltip behavior with metric-row semantic boundaries instead of treating all pointer sweeps as equally suppressible.

tasks.title: Run-column-aware comparison tooltip sweep suppression shipped
tasks.description: Completed the run-column sweep suppression follow-up by extending metric-cell pointer sampling to track both metric row and run column keys, splitting sweep suppression into separate same-row horizontal and same-column vertical contexts, and adding a distinct column-scan interaction profile so fast downward scans within one run column damp tooltip swaps without reusing the exact row-sweep timing.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the run-column-aware sweep suppression in `App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `2a68def` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed row-aware sweep suppression by separating vertical run-column inspection from horizontal metric-row scanning, letting reference-lane comparison tables tune each high-density interaction pattern independently.

tasks.title: Tune comparison tooltip suppression asymmetrically for downward versus upward column scans
tasks.description: Completed the asymmetric vertical sweep follow-up by splitting same-column comparison tooltip suppression into distinct `column_down` and `column_up` states, applying a faster tooltip handoff profile for downward scans while keeping upward scans more conservative, and preserving the completed row-sweep, keyboard, and non-metric cue behavior.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the asymmetric column-sweep tuning in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `5507758` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed run-column-aware sweep suppression by tuning vertical comparison-table scan behavior to the likely top-to-bottom operator review pattern instead of treating upward and downward column sweeps identically.

tasks.title: Adapt comparison tooltip sweep thresholds to cell size or pointer speed
tasks.description: Completed the adaptive-threshold follow-up by capturing metric-cell dimensions in recent pointer samples and deriving row/column sweep suppression thresholds from observed pointer speed plus average cell size, so narrow cells react faster while larger or slower interactions require stronger evidence before tooltip swaps are damped.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the adaptive comparison tooltip threshold implementation in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `6064d92` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed asymmetric column-sweep tuning by replacing hard-coded dense-table suppression thresholds with interaction-aware scaling that can better match different comparison layouts and operator scan speeds.

tasks.title: Dev-only tuning panel for comparison tooltip sweep thresholds shipped
tasks.description: Completed the sweep-calibration follow-up by adding a development-only tuning panel for comparison tooltip suppression, moving the relevant row/column delay, hold, and adaptive threshold values into live UI state, and wiring the panel plus reset action so dense-table behavior can be tuned without source edits.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the dev-only tuning panel in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` plus `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `40b7276` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed adaptive threshold logic by giving the reference-lane comparison UI a practical calibration surface for dense-table tooltip behavior before committing to longer-term operator defaults.

tasks.title: Dev tooltip tuning presets now persist for repeatable comparison sessions
tasks.description: Completed the dev-only tooltip tuning persistence follow-up by storing the current draft tuning values, named presets, and selected preset in `localStorage`, adding save/load/delete preset controls to the tuning panel, and restoring persisted data through versioned normalization so malformed or outdated browser state falls back safely to default tuning.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the preset persistence work in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `bdcab63` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference-lane tooltip calibration loop from one-off local tuning toward repeatable operator comparisons by making chosen sweep settings durable or shareable across sessions.

tasks.title: Tooltip tuning preset import/export and URL sharing shipped
tasks.description: Completed the portability follow-up by adding dev-only JSON export/import for the current tuning bundle plus saved presets, generating URL-backed share payloads for the same bundle, hydrating shared tuning state from the URL on load, and preserving versioned normalization so malformed or incompatible payloads fall back safely instead of breaking the tuning panel.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the tooltip preset portability work in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `d2961f3` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed local preset persistence by making reference-lane tooltip calibration profiles portable across operators and machines instead of remaining browser-local.

tasks.title: Support sharing or exporting a single named tooltip tuning preset
tasks.description: Completed the tooltip tuning portability refinement by adding selected-preset-only share URLs and standalone JSON export from the dev-only panel, extending import parsing so single-preset payloads merge into the current bundle while full bundles still replace it, and separating bundle-versus-selected-preset controls in the panel copy and layout.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the single-preset portability follow-up in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `9cba3a8` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed bundle-level portability flow by making reference-lane tooltip calibration sharing more targeted and practical for iterative preset comparisons.

tasks.title: Handle preset name conflicts when importing shared tooltip tuning presets
tasks.description: Completed the tooltip preset conflict-handling follow-up by adding single-preset import conflict policy controls in the dev tuning panel, supporting `rename` and `overwrite` outcomes for duplicate preset names, defaulting shared preset URL loads to safe rename behavior, and normalizing imported JSON to the actual stored preset name when a rename occurs.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the preset import conflict-handling work in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `d1caf5a` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the completed bundle and single-preset portability flow by making reference-lane tooltip preset imports safer when shared payloads collide with existing local preset names.

tasks.title: Interactive tooltip preset import conflict resolution shipped
tasks.description: Completed the dev-only tooltip tuning import refinement by replacing the advance conflict policy selector with inline conflict handling that appears only on actual preset-name collisions, offering `Rename and import`, `Overwrite existing`, and `Skip import`, prefilling an import-safe rename suggestion, and keeping shared preset URL loads on non-destructive safe-rename behavior outside the manual import flow.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the inline collision-resolution flow in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `30a1dfe` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Closed the reference-lane tooltip preset import follow-up by moving collision handling from a global preselection model to a targeted operator decision at the moment an actual name collision occurs.

tasks.title: Tooltip preset import conflicts now preview existing versus incoming values
tasks.description: Completed the tooltip preset conflict-preview follow-up by showing existing and incoming preset values side by side inside the inline conflict card, summarizing how many tuning values differ versus match, and highlighting changed rows in a compact comparison table before the operator chooses rename, overwrite, or skip.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the tooltip preset conflict-preview refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `93e90d9` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the completed interactive collision workflow by giving reference-lane operators the value-level context needed to choose rename or overwrite confidently when shared tooltip presets collide.

tasks.title: Show changed tooltip preset values first and collapse unchanged rows behind a toggle
tasks.description: Completed the inline tooltip preset conflict-preview scanability follow-up by sorting changed values ahead of unchanged rows, collapsing unchanged rows behind an explicit reveal toggle, resetting the toggle when a new conflict payload loads, and adding supporting inline toggle styling in the dev conflict preview.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the changed-first and collapsed-unchanged conflict-preview refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `fff37ff` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the completed value-level conflict preview by making reference-lane import comparisons faster to scan when only a small subset of tuning values actually differ.

tasks.title: Add stronger visual diff cues to tooltip preset conflict rows
tasks.description: Completed the row-level conflict-visibility follow-up by adding per-row delta metadata, direction badges, formatted delta labels, left accent stripes, direction-colored badges, separate existing versus incoming value chips, and stronger emphasis on incoming changed values in the tooltip preset conflict preview.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the stronger row-level diff-cue refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `6d4d629` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the changed-first conflict preview by making value deltas themselves easier to interpret at a glance when reference-lane tooltip preset imports disagree.

tasks.title: Tooltip preset conflict rows now grouped by setting type
tasks.description: Completed the conflict-preview grouping follow-up by organizing preset comparison rows into explicit setting categories such as hover timing, sweep detection, row sweep, column sweep directions, and speed scaling, while preserving the existing changed-first flow within each category and adding section headers for both immediately visible changed rows and toggle-revealed unchanged rows.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the grouped conflict-preview refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `8e991a1` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference-lane conflict-preview usability work by restructuring tooltip preset parameters into operator-meaningful groups once the most immediate diff visibility refinement is in place.

tasks.title: Add group-level diff summaries to tooltip preset conflict categories
tasks.description: Completed the grouped-summary follow-up by adding compact category header summaries for tooltip preset conflict groups, surfacing changed counts plus higher/lower or match breakdowns where relevant across both immediately visible changed categories and unchanged categories revealed by the global toggle, and updating the grouped header styling for readable two-part labels.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the group-level diff summary refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `6ee0bca` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds directly on the completed grouped conflict preview by adding section-level scanning cues that help operators prioritize the most materially changed tooltip tuning categories first.

tasks.title: Add per-category collapse controls for large unchanged tooltip conflict groups
tasks.description: Completed the unchanged-group refinement by adding category-level collapse controls in the tooltip preset conflict preview, default-collapsing large unchanged categories once the global unchanged section is opened, resetting per-category collapse state when a new conflict payload loads, and adding supporting per-group control styling.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-category unchanged-group collapse controls in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `7b85a59` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed grouped-summary conflict preview by letting operators keep large unchanged categories collapsed independently, reducing noise without losing the category-level context added by the new summaries.

tasks.title: Preserve per-category unchanged group expansion while toggling the global unchanged section
tasks.description: Completed the unchanged-section toggle follow-up by preserving each category's expanded or collapsed state across global `Show/Hide unchanged` toggles, seeding the default collapse map only on the first reopen for the current conflict payload, and keeping per-category inspection choices intact until the conflict payload itself changes.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the global-toggle preservation refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `bb3ea28` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed unchanged-group collapse controls by making the tooltip preset conflict preview feel stable during repeated global show or hide toggles while operators inspect specific unchanged categories.

tasks.title: Unchanged-category expansion choices now persist per conflict session across dev panel reopen
tasks.description: Completed the per-conflict-session persistence follow-up by storing dev-only conflict preview UI state in local storage keyed by conflict session, restoring both the unchanged-section open state and per-category expanded or collapsed choices when the same conflict session returns after the dev panel is reopened, and preserving saved choices by seeding default collapse state only for groups without stored session state.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-conflict-session unchanged-group persistence refinement in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `feb663f` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the completed unchanged-group toggle preservation by extending tooltip preset conflict-preview continuity across dev-panel close or reopen cycles for the same imported conflict session.

tasks.title: Add explicit reset controls for saved tooltip conflict-view state
tasks.description: Completed the saved conflict-view recovery follow-up by adding explicit dev-only reset controls that clear remembered unchanged-section and per-category expansion choices either for the active conflict session or for every persisted conflict session, while keeping the conflict-view persistence and reset logic local to `ComparisonTooltipTuningPanel` and routing feedback messaging through the parent.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the explicit conflict-view reset controls in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `3fd1df4` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the new per-conflict-session conflict-preview persistence by giving operators an explicit recovery path when remembered dev-panel state should be discarded for one session or globally.

tasks.title: Add confirmation before clearing all saved tooltip conflict views
tasks.description: Completed the destructive-reset guardrail follow-up by changing the dev-only `Reset all saved views` action into an inline confirmation flow with explicit confirm and cancel actions, auto-closing the warning state once no saved conflict-view sessions remain, and adding matching warning styling so operators do not accidentally wipe every persisted tooltip conflict-view session.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the all-sessions reset confirmation flow in `apps/web/src/App.tsx` and `apps/web/src/styles.css`, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `36f5417` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the shipped conflict-view reset controls by adding a safer operator guardrail around the destructive all-sessions reset path without reopening the scoped current-session reset flow.

tasks.title: Saved tooltip conflict reset summary shipped
tasks.description: Completed the reset-summary follow-up by listing which saved tooltip conflict sessions will be cleared inside the inline confirmation card, grouping remembered sessions by imported preset with counts, marking the currently active session when included, and capping the visible list with an overflow note so operators can verify destructive reset scope at a glance.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the saved-session reset summary in the inline confirmation card, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `81bcdf7` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the shipped destructive-reset confirmation by making the all-sessions clear action more legible about exactly which persisted conflict-view sessions are about to be removed.

tasks.title: On-demand expansion for the full saved reset list shipped
tasks.description: Completed the reset-list overflow follow-up by adding inline `Show all ... preset groups` and `Show fewer preset groups` controls to the destructive reset confirmation card, keeping the preset-group summary capped by default, and automatically resetting the expanded state when the card closes or no overflow remains.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the reset-list expansion refinement in the tooltip conflict clear-confirmation card, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `fa57794` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the shipped reset-summary card by preserving the default compact view while letting operators inspect every saved conflict-view session before a destructive reset.

tasks.title: Per-group expansion within the saved reset summary shipped
tasks.description: Completed the reset-summary detail follow-up by adding per-preset-group `Show ... sessions` / `Hide sessions` controls inside the destructive reset confirmation card, revealing each saved session entry with short session ids plus a `current` marker when expanded, and resetting per-group expansion state whenever the confirmation card closes or reopens.
tasks.priority_reason: Main session completed the per-group reset-summary expansion refinement in the tooltip conflict clear-confirmation card, verified `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` in `apps/web`, and pushed commit `b884c27` to `origin/main`.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the shipped reset-summary expansion card by preserving the compact grouped overview while letting operators inspect individual preset groups in detail before a destructive reset.

tasks.title: Show clearer per-session metadata in the saved reset summary
tasks.description: Refine the `Reset all saved views` confirmation summary so expanded saved sessions show more informative per-session metadata than a short saved-session id alone, such as when the session was first stored or which conflict layout choices it remembers.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply said nothing essential remains for the completed per-group expansion task and explicitly identified clearer per-session metadata than the short saved-session id as the likeliest next refinement, making it the clearest immediately executable follow-up.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Builds on the shipped per-group reset-summary expansion by making the saved-session entries themselves more legible, so operators can distinguish remembered conflict-view sessions by meaningful metadata before a destructive reset.

tasks.title: Evaluate narrower reset scopes for saved tooltip conflict views
tasks.description: Explore narrower conflict-view reset scopes, such as clearing only one preset group or one conflict context, so operators can recover from stale remembered state without always choosing between current-session-only and wipe-everything reset behavior.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply named narrower reset scopes as a plausible follow-up, but the exact scope boundaries still need product judgment after the simpler confirmation guardrail is addressed.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the conflict-view recovery path toward more granular reset controls if the new current-session versus all-sessions split still proves too coarse in practice.

tasks.title: Add expand-all and collapse-all controls for unchanged tooltip conflict categories
tasks.description: Provide bulk expand or collapse controls for unchanged tooltip conflict categories so operators can open or close every unchanged category in one action when they need a full scan instead of one-by-one inspection.
tasks.priority_reason: Bulk expand-all or collapse-all controls remain a plausible refinement for large unchanged groups, but the latest main-session reply instead pointed to reset controls for the newly persisted conflict-view state as the more direct follow-up after session-level persistence shipped.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the unchanged-category conflict-preview controls from per-group inspection toward faster bulk review when operators want to open or close the entire unchanged section at once.

tasks.title: Persist lightweight telemetry for comparison tooltip sweep behavior
tasks.description: Capture lightweight comparison-table interaction telemetry around tooltip suppression decisions, pointer speed bands, cell dimensions, and swap outcomes so the adaptive sweep heuristics can be tuned from observed operator behavior instead of only from hand-tuned constants.
tasks.priority_reason: The latest main-session reply named lightweight interaction telemetry as a plausible next refinement, but it is a broader instrumentation choice than the immediately actionable dev-only tuning panel and may need product judgment on storage scope and retention.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Extends the reference-lane tooltip calibration work from local manual tuning toward evidence-backed threshold decisions using real comparison-table interaction patterns.
